pi_items.source: Verified in task.json description, setup_task.sh, export_result.sh, and verifier.py - all reference the same company name for adding to registry
Verified in task.json description states 'select India (IN)', verifier.py line 49 defines india_codes={'IN', 'IND', 'INDIA'}, setup_task.sh line 74 states 'country: India/IN'
Verified in task.json description 'using username admin' and setup_task.sh line 76 'Login: admin / adminpass123'
Verified in task.json description 'password adminpass123' and setup_task.sh line 76 'Login: admin / adminpass123'
Verified in task.json description, task.json metadata, and setup_task.sh which launches Firefox to this URL

Task description mentions 'In the Registry section, find Companys and click + Add', which would lead to the /admin/registry/company/add/ path. This follows standard Django admin URL patterns for adding objects.

privileged_info_summary: This is a synthetic task to add a manufacturer to Aerobridge, an open-source drone fleet management system. The task requires adding a company named 'SkyTech Innovations' (country: IN/India) to the registry via the Django admin panel at http://localhost:8000/admin/ using credentials admin/adminpass123. All metadata claims are consistent across the task definition, setup scripts, export scripts, and verifier. The verifier checks that the company was successfully added with the correct name and country code.
